description_long: This object consists of two mushrooms with large, flat, red caps adorned with prominent white circular spots. The caps are broad and slightly polygonal, with the larger mushroom's cap overlapping the smaller one. Both mushrooms have thick, cylindrical stems that are a pale grayish-white color, with the larger stem supporting the upper cap and the smaller stem extending outward at an angle. The surface of the caps appears smooth and matte, with the white spots providing a striking contrast against the deep red background. The gills beneath the caps are not visible in any view, and the mushrooms are depicted as a group, suggesting a natural cluster. The overall appearance is reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in parks or grassy urban areas.
description: A pair of red-capped mushrooms with white spots and thick, pale stems, clustered together. The caps are broad and smooth, with no visible gills underneath.
description_view_0: The view shows the tops of both mushroom caps, highlighting the red color and white spots, with the stems mostly hidden.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the side of the larger mushroom and the full length of the smaller mushroom's stem, showing the cluster arrangement.
description_view_2: A side view displaying both stems clearly, with the larger mushroom standing upright and the smaller one angled outward.
description_view_3: The underside of the smaller mushroom's cap and its stem are visible, but no gills are seen; the caps' spotted pattern is prominent.
